title: Parse HTTP Response
description:
Given a raw HTTP response (status line + headers + blank line + body), extract key fields.

Return four lines:
```
status N
content_type VALUE
request_id VALUE
body_length N
```

Use `none` if `X-Request-Id` is not present. `body_length` is the number of bytes in the body (after the blank line separating headers from body).
constraints: Headers are case-insensitive — normalise to lowercase for matching
The blank line separating headers from body contains only \r\n or \n
body_length is the byte length of the body, not the Content-Length header value
Return `none` if X-Request-Id header is absent

examples.input:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json
X-Request-Id: abc-123
Content-Length: 45

{"status":"ok"}
examples.output:
status 200
content_type application/json
request_id abc-123
body_length 15
examples.explanation: Status 200, content-type and request-id extracted, body is 14 bytes

test_cases.input:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json
X-Request-Id: abc-123
Content-Length: 45

{"status":"ok"}
test_cases.expected_output:
status 200
content_type application/json
request_id abc-123
body_length 15

test_cases.input:
HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
Content-Type: text/plain

error occurred
test_cases.expected_output:
status 500
content_type text/plain
request_id none
body_length 14

test_cases.input:
HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently
Content-Type: text/html
X-Request-Id: xyz-789
Location: https://new.example.com
test_cases.expected_output:
status 301
content_type text/html
request_id xyz-789
body_length 0
